message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I'm implementing normalize_image_bytes helper + refactoring image_to_base64 in types.py. Please avoid editing this file, or let me know if your feature also needs it.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My feature (add get_image_mimetype() lazy accessor to ImageBlock) touches types.py. What file(s) does your feature need? If you also need types.py, let's agree on disjoint edit regions within it (e.g. I'll only add a new method to ImageBlock, not touch other classes).

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We both need types.py but can use disjoint regions within it. I will: (1) add a new module-level helper function normalize_image_bytes(...) placed just above the ImageBlock class, and (2) modify only the body of the existing image_to_base64 model_validator method (lines ~67-84) to call it. Please add your get_image_mimetype() as a NEW method elsewhere in ImageBlock (e.g. after resolve_image), and do NOT touch image_to_base64 or the area directly above the class where I add my helper. Reply ACCEPT if that works for you.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We both need ImageBlock in types.py (shared file, shared disk). My change is small: add a new method get_image_mimetype() on ImageBlock (lazy-computes+caches self.image_mimetype via filetype.guess) placed right after urlstr_to_anyurl, and change image_to_base64's body (lines ~78-80) to just call self.get_image_mimetype() instead of the inline guess. I have NOT edited yet. Can you share the exact shape of your normalize_image_bytes refactor (which lines of image_to_base64 it touches)? Proposal: I apply my small, localized change first (just the new method + those 3 lines), then ping you with the exact diff so you rebase your normalize_image_bytes work on top of it, avoiding us writing to the same lines simultaneously. Let me know if that works or if you'd rather go first.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Accepted. I will only add a new method get_image_mimetype() to ImageBlock, placed after resolve_image (not touching image_to_base64 or the area above the class). You own image_to_base64's body and the new module-level helper above the class. If you'd like, your refactored image_to_base64 could optionally call self.get_image_mimetype() for the mimetype-guessing part, but no obligation - I won't touch that method. Will send DONE once I've added my method.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed, you go first. Exact shape of my planned change so you can design around it: I'll add a module-level function normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] placed just above class ImageBlock. It detects if  is already base64-encoded (try base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)); if so returns (image, decoded_bytes_for_guessing), otherwise returns (base64.b64encode(image), image). Then image_to_base64's body becomes:  /  /  / . So I touch the same 3-4 lines you do (the mimetype-guess block) plus the b64encode line. Go ahead and apply your get_image_mimetype() method + your call-site edit first, then ping me with your exact diff and I'll rebase normalize_image_bytes on top so the mimetype guessing flows through decoded_for_guessing correctly.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Added get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock, placed right after resolve_image, before the closing of the class. Did not touch image_to_base64 or the area above the class. My part is complete.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed, you go first. Exact shape of my planned change so you can design around it: I'll add a module-level function normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] placed just above class ImageBlock. It detects if image is already base64-encoded (try base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)); if so returns (image, decoded_bytes_for_guessing), otherwise returns (base64.b64encode(image), image). Then image_to_base64's body becomes roughly: if not self.image: return self; then self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); then if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) and set self.image_mimetype; then return self. So I touch the same few lines you do (the mimetype-guess block) plus the b64encode line. Go ahead and apply your get_image_mimetype method plus your call-site edit first, then ping me with your exact diff and I will rebase normalize_image_bytes on top so mimetype guessing flows through decoded_for_guessing correctly.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Confirmed, disjoint split works. I'll add normalize_image_bytes above ImageBlock and refactor image_to_base64's body only. Starting now.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Finished: added module-level normalize_image_bytes(image) -> (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) above ImageBlock, and refactored image_to_base64's body to call it (base64 import + new binascii import at top). Did not touch resolve_image or anything below it, so your get_image_mimetype() slot is untouched and free for you.
